info.name: Hookbase API - Integration Tests
info.description:
End-to-end integration tests for the Hookbase API. Run the entire collection in order — requests create resources, capture IDs/slugs, and use them in subsequent requests.

Setup: Set the `apiKey` variable to your API key before running.

Flow: Health → Sources → Destinations → Filters → Transforms → Schemas → Routes → Ingest Webhook → Events → Deliveries → Tunnels → Cron Groups → Cron Jobs → Notification Channels → Analytics → Audit Logs → API Keys → Cleanup

A random `testRun` suffix is generated once per collection run to avoid conflicts.

exec: // Generate a unique testRun suffix once per collection run
pm.test('Response has source with id, slug, and ingestUrl', function () {
pm.test('Response has sources array and pagination', function () {
pm.test('Response has destination with id and slug', function () {
pm.test('Response has destinations array and pagination', function () {
pm.test('Response has filter with id and slug', function () {
pm.test('Response has transform with id and slug', function () {
pm.test('Response has schema with id and slug', function () {
pm.test('Response has route with id', function () {
pm.test('Response has events array with at least one event', function () {
pm.test('Capture eventId from first event', function () {
pm.test('Response has tunnel with id', function () {
pm.test('Response has group with id and slug', function () {
pm.test('Response has cron job with id', function () {
pm.test('Response has cron job data', function () {
// Notification channels may be feature-gated (paid plans)
pm.test('Response has channel with id', function () {
// Dashboard may return stats, overview, or full dashboard object
// Audit logs may be feature-gated for non-admin users
pm.test('Get audit log actions returns 200', function () {
// Clear testRun so next collection run generates a new suffix
pm.test('Health check returns 200 (final sanity check)', function () {
